draft.introduction: The New Covenant prophecy, the longest OT passage quoted in the New Testament (Hebrews 8:8-12). Jeremiah envisions a covenant written on hearts rather than stone tablets.

translation.text: Behold, the days are coming, declares the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and the house of Judah. I will put my law within them, and I will write it on their hearts. And I will be their God, and they shall be my people. For they shall all know me, from the least of them to the greatest.
translation.original_text: ἰδοὺ ἡμέραι ἔρχονται φησὶν κύριος καὶ διαθήσομαι διαθήκην καινήν

translator_notes.note: The term διαθήκην καινήν (“new covenant”) is the origin of the Christian term “New Testament.” Jesus echoes this language at the Last Supper: “This cup is the new covenant in my blood” (Luke 22:20).
